description: Validates that stg_employees contains exactly 14 columns as specified in the ICD, no more and no fewer.
precondition: stg_employees table must exist and be accessible in the target BigQuery dataset prior to test execution.
steps: 1. Query INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS filtered to stg_employees  2. Count total rows returned  3. Compare result to ICD-defined count of 14  4. Fail and log if count differs
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS column_count FROM <project>.<dataset>.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees'
sql_hint: COUNT(*) FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' — expect 14
linked_ac: Schema compatibility is validated before each load execution and mismatches are logged

test_name: Column Names And Data Types Match
description: Validates that every column name and its data type in stg_employees exactly matches the 14-column ICD specification with all columns typed as STRING.
precondition: stg_employees table must exist in the target BigQuery dataset and INFORMATION_SCHEMA must be queryable by the test service account.
input_data: ICD column set: EMPLOYEE_ID STRING, FIRST_NAME STRING, LAST_NAME STRING, EMAIL STRING, PHONE_NUMBER STRING, HIRE_DATE STRING, JOB_ID STRING, SALARY STRING, COMMISSION_PCT STRING, MANAGER_ID STRING, DEPARTMENT_ID STRING, STATUS STRING, CREATED_DATE STRING, UPDATED_DATE STRING
steps: 1. Query INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS for stg_employees returning column_name and data_type  2. Anti-join result against ICD-defined (column_name, data_type) pairs  3. Assert zero rows in mismatch result set  4. Log any returned rows as schema drift findings
query: SELECT column_name, data_type FROM <project>.<dataset>.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE table_name = 'stg_employees' AND (column_name, data_type) NOT IN (('EMPLOYEE_ID','STRING'),('FIRST_NAME','STRING'),('LAST_NAME','STRING'),('EMAIL','STRING'),('PHONE_NUMBER','STRING'),('HIRE_DATE','STRING'),('JOB_ID','STRING'),('SALARY','STRING'),('COMMISSION_PCT','STRING'),('MANAGER_ID','STRING'),('DEPARTMENT_ID','STRING'),('STATUS','STRING'),('CREATED_DATE','STRING'),('UPDATED_DATE','STRING'))
sql_hint: INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE (column_name, data_type) NOT IN (<14 ICD pairs>) — expects 0 mismatch rows
expected_result: 0 rows returned; no column name or data type deviation from ICD
linked_ac: Schema compatibility is validated before each load execution and mismatches are logged

description: Validate that the EMAIL column, defined as NOT NULL in the MySQL source, contains zero NULL values in BigQuery after a full or incremental load.
precondition: stg_employees table has been successfully loaded from MySQL source and contains at least one row.
steps: 1. Execute COUNT query filtering EMAIL IS NULL against stg_employees  2. Retrieve null_email_count scalar result  3. Assert null_email_count equals 0  4. If count > 0 log offending EMPLOYEE_IDs and fail the validation gate
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS null_email_count FROM <project>.<dataset>.stg_employees WHERE EMAIL IS NULL
sql_hint: COUNT(*) WHERE EMAIL IS NULL — expects 0 rows; any result > 0 blocks load acceptance
expected_result: null_email_count = 0; EMAIL column has no NULL values
linked_ac: AC: Null and datatype constraint violations detected during validation block load completion before data reaches BigQuery

description: Validate that the FIRST_NAME column contains no empty string or whitespace-only values after load, as blank names indicate a source extraction or transformation defect.
precondition: stg_employees table has been successfully loaded and contains records where FIRST_NAME is expected to carry a non-blank PII name value from MySQL source.
steps: 1. Execute COUNT query applying TRIM(FIRST_NAME) = '' against stg_employees  2. Retrieve empty_firstname_count scalar result  3. Assert empty_firstname_count equals 0  4. If count > 0 log offending EMPLOYEE_IDs and raise a data-quality alert
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS empty_firstname_count FROM <project>.<dataset>.stg_employees WHERE TRIM(COALESCE(FIRST_NAME, 'X')) = ''
sql_hint: COUNT(*) WHERE TRIM(FIRST_NAME) = '' — COALESCE guards against NULL short-circuit; expects 0 rows
expected_result: empty_firstname_count = 0; no empty or whitespace-only values present in FIRST_NAME
linked_ac: AC: Null and datatype constraint violations detected during validation block load completion before data reaches BigQuery
